title: Delicate Algerian Casabe Flatbread
description: A floral, crunchy wood‑fired flatbread made with casabe flour, orange zest, and edible flowers, perfect for midnight cravings.
instructions: In a bowl, mix casabe flour and sea salt until combined.
Gradually whisk in water and olive oil until a firm dough forms.
Stir in chopped rosemary, orange zest, and lemon juice for flavor.
Let the dough rest for 10 minutes to hydrate.
Heat a wood‑fired oven to 250C or preheat a cast iron skillet on high heat.
Divide the dough into 6 equal portions and roll each into a thin circle about 30cm diameter.
Place the flatbreads onto a preheated stone or skillet and bake 3 to 4 minutes per side until crisp and lightly browned.
While still hot, brush each flatbread with a thin layer of olive oil.
Sprinkle black sesame seeds and edible flowers over the top for garnish.
Slice into squares and serve immediately as a midnight snack.
